summary: Do not ship the plan yet: R-1 and R-3 are addressed, but R-2 remains only partially fixed because the acceptance matrix still omits release-critical stories.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: R-2 remains: acceptance matrix omits critical retained behaviors
findings.body: The matrix claims to map user stories to observable tests, but it provides no acceptance coverage for stories 18–22, 25, or 28: profile-header editing, status summary, localized compile warnings, one-click pause/resume, popup management actions, cross-surface editor parity, and en/ko catalog enforcement. The generic statement that existing smoke scenarios remain does not identify whether these relocated controls and contracts are covered. A competent implementer could complete the listed matrix while silently dropping or breaking these explicitly retained behaviors.
findings.recommendation: Add matrix rows with concrete seams and observable assertions for stories 18–22, 25, and 28. Where existing smoke or type checks already cover them, name the exact scenarios/checks and specify the updated interaction path.

next_steps: Complete the acceptance matrix for the omitted stories, then rerun the round-2 gate.
